issue.title: FRS alert: vertical-farm nutrient pumps are failing intermittently
issue.description: This issue did not start with a person — the Feedback & Review System raised it. FRS detected an intermittent failure pattern in the vertical farm's nutrient-pump system and submitted a signal to the CDS. Two solid remediations are on the table; the node must choose.

context.historical: Pumps installed 2022; three pressure-loss events in the last 10 days, trend worsening (FRS signal).
context.ecological: A new controller adds electronics/e-waste; repair-first reuses existing hardware.
context.resources: Spare pump parts are in stock; a new automated controller is an external-dependency purchase.
context.labor: About 90 skilled labor-hours available; either fix fits the window.
context.dependencies: The vertical farm is the node's main fresh-food source; pump failure risks a whole grow cycle.

proposals.label: Repair pumps and add redundancy
proposals.summary: Repair the failing pumps and add a parallel backup line using in-stock parts.

proposals.label: Replace with a new automated controller
proposals.summary: Swap the system for a new automated hydroponic controller with self-diagnostics.

proposals.label: Monitor for now, no intervention
proposals.summary: Bridge-step option: keep monitoring via FRS and intervene only if failures escalate.

constraints.name: ecological_budget
constraints.message: Embodied-carbon / e-waste impact exceeds the node's ecological budget.

intake.author: stillwater:frs-monitor
intake.text: FRS alert: nutrient-pump pressure dropped below threshold 3 times in 10 days; intermittent failure pattern, trend worsening.

intake.author: stillwater:maya
intake.text: The pumps keep cutting out mid-cycle and we lose a whole nutrient batch.

intake.author: stillwater:devon
intake.text: The pumps keep cutting out mid-cycle and crews lose a whole shift.

intake.author: stillwater:sam
intake.text: Whatever we choose, mind the cost — a full controller swap isn't cheap on parts.

intake.author: stillwater:tomas
intake.text: A new controller is more electronics to e-waste later; repair-first is greener if it'll hold.
